effects_on_skin.collagen_synthesis: E2-ERα binding increases procollagen type I and III mRNA expression; stimulates TGF-β1; inhibits MMP-1 and MMP-3; increases TIMP-1 and TIMP-3. Increases skin collagen content by 2–5% per year in postmenopausal women; skin thickness increases 7–15%; dermal elasticity improves 20–30%.
effects_on_skin.hyaluronic_acid: Upregulates HAS2 in fibroblasts and keratinocytes; increases HA in dermis for water retention, turgor, volume.
effects_on_skin.barrier_function: Increases stratum corneum hydration via enhanced HA, ceramide synthesis, and tight junction proteins (claudin-1, occludin).
effects_on_skin.vascularity: Improves cutaneous blood flow via ERα in vascular endothelium.
effects_on_skin.wound_healing: Supports inflammation, collagen synthesis, and cell migration via HA.

testing.dutch_test: Measures E1, E2, E3, and estrogen metabolites (2-OH, 4-OH, 16α-OH); assesses metabolism pathways
testing.estriol_e3: Pregnancy estrogen; minimal in non-pregnant women

effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Binds androgen receptors in sebaceous glands with lower affinity than DHT; activates 5α-reductase; stimulates sebocyte proliferation and lipid synthesis. Increases sebum 30–50% in luteal phase.
effects_on_skin.cyclical_acne: Luteal phase rise (days 15–28) drives premenstrual acne flares, especially jawline/chin.
effects_on_skin.melanocyte_stimulation: Contributes to melasma risk alongside estrogen.

testing.serum_progesterone: Day 21 (7 days post-ovulation); confirms ovulation and luteal phase adequacy
testing.dutch_test: Measures total daily progesterone production and metabolites

effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Converted to DHT by 5α-reductase; DHT has 5× higher AR affinity; primary driver of sebum production. Men produce 10× more testosterone than women.
effects_on_skin.acne: Drives sebum overproduction → comedone formation; upregulates TLR2 on sebocytes; promotes keratinocyte proliferation.
effects_on_skin.hirsutism: Converts vellus to terminal hairs in androgen-sensitive areas (upper lip, chin, chest, abdomen, back, inner thighs).
effects_on_skin.hair_loss: DHT binds AR in scalp dermal papilla → follicle miniaturization; shortens anagen phase.

testing.total_testosterone: AM, fasting; 6–45 ng/dL (women); >55 suggests PCOS or tumor
testing.free_testosterone: AM; 0.2–1.4 ng/dL (women); more accurate if SHBG abnormal
testing.dht: Active androgen in skin; directly drives sebum, acne, hair
testing.dutch_test: Measures testosterone and DHT metabolites (androsterone, etiocholanolone)

effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Primary androgen for sebaceous glands; 5× higher AR affinity than testosterone; drives lipogenic enzymes (FAS, ACC, SCD).
effects_on_skin.acne: Directly upregulates TLR2 on sebocytes; enhances inflammatory response to C. acnes; promotes follicular hyperkeratinization.
effects_on_skin.hair_loss: Binds AR in scalp dermal papilla → increases TGF-β1/β2 → catagen promotion; reduces Wnt/β-catenin signaling.
effects_on_skin.hirsutism: Converts vellus to terminal hair in androgen-sensitive areas.

testing.dutch_test: Measures DHT and 5α-reductase activity via metabolites

effects_on_skin.collagen_breakdown: Chronic elevation upregulates MMP-1, MMP-3, MMP-9; degrades collagen types I and III; suppresses collagen synthesis by 30–40%.
effects_on_skin.barrier_disruption: Reduces keratinocyte proliferation; downregulates filaggrin, loricrin, involucrin; disrupts lipid lamellae; increases TEWL.
effects_on_skin.inflammation: Acute: anti-inflammatory. Chronic: paradoxically pro-inflammatory via glucocorticoid resistance, insulin resistance, Th2 dominance.
effects_on_skin.delayed_wound_healing: Suppresses neutrophil/macrophage recruitment, fibroblast proliferation, VEGF production, and collagen synthesis.
effects_on_skin.skin_thinning: Suppresses epidermal and dermal cell turnover; causes atrophy with chronic exposure.

testing.serum_cortisol: AM (8 AM): 5–25 mcg/dL; PM (4 PM): lower; midnight nadir
testing.dutch_test: 4-point urine cortisol curve + cortisone; cortisol:cortisone ratio indicates 11β-HSD activity

effects_on_skin.metabolic_rate: T3 > T4 regulates basal metabolic rate in keratinocytes, fibroblasts, adnexal structures.
effects_on_skin.cell_turnover: Controls proliferation and differentiation of skin cells.

testing.tsh: 0.4–4.0 mIU/L; >2.5 with symptoms may warrant treatment
testing.free_t4: 0.8–1.8 ng/dL; primary hypothyroidism: high TSH, low free T4
testing.free_t3: 2.3–4.2 pg/mL; low with normal T4 = conversion issue
testing.tg_antibodies: <40 IU/mL; elevated in autoimmune thyroid disease

effects_on_skin.glycation_and_ages: Hyperinsulinemia → elevated glucose → non-enzymatic glycation → AGEs on collagen and elastin → cross-linking, stiffness, yellowing.
effects_on_skin.acne_pathway: High glycemic load → insulin spike → increased hepatic IGF-1; decreases IGFBP-3 → more free IGF-1; reduces SHBG → more free testosterone.
effects_on_skin.mTORC1_activation: Insulin/IGF-1 activates mTORC1 → SREBP-1 → lipogenesis; keratinocyte proliferation; AR signaling.
effects_on_skin.inflammation: RAGE signaling → NF-κB → IL-6, TNF-α, MMP-9.

testing.homa_ir: <2.5; calculated from fasting glucose and insulin

effects_on_skin.cell_proliferation: Stimulates keratinocyte, fibroblast, and sebocyte proliferation via IGF-1R.
effects_on_skin.collagen_synthesis: Promotes fibroblast activity and collagen production.
effects_on_skin.wound_healing: Supports keratinocyte migration, angiogenesis, and collagen deposition.
effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Synergizes with androgens on sebocytes; drives acne.
effects_on_skin.hair_growth: IGF-1 in dermal papilla promotes anagen maintenance.

testing.serum_igf_1: Age-adjusted reference ranges; elevated in insulin resistance and GH excess
testing.igfbp_3: IGF-binding protein 3; low levels increase free IGF-1

effects_on_skin.skin_thickness: Stimulates fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis via IGF-1 mediation.
effects_on_skin.wound_healing: Promotes keratinocyte migration, angiogenesis, collagen deposition via IGF-1.
effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Indirectly via IGF-1; excess can worsen acne.

testing.igf_1: Primary screening test for GH status

effects_on_skin.antioxidant: Potent free radical scavenger (hydroxyl, peroxynitrite, singlet oxygen); stimulates SOD, catalase, glutathione peroxidase; more effective than vitamin E in some models.
effects_on_skin.circadian_repair: Binds MT1/MT2 receptors in skin cells; triggers nocturnal repair: increased cell proliferation, DNA repair, collagen synthesis, barrier recovery.
effects_on_skin.uv_protection: Topical application reduces UV-induced erythema, edema, sunburn cell formation; synergizes with vitamins C and E.
effects_on_skin.mitochondrial_protection: Protects mitochondrial DNA from oxidative damage; reduces lipid peroxidation.

effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Converts to testosterone and DHT in sebaceous glands; drives sebocyte activity.
effects_on_skin.skin_aging: Declines 10% per decade after age 30 (adrenopause); correlated with reduced collagen, thickness, and hydration.
effects_on_skin.wound_healing: Supports skin cell proliferation and repair via androgen and estrogen metabolites.
effects_on_skin.hair_and_acne: Contributes to acne, hirsutism, and hair loss when elevated.

testing.dhea_s: AM; 35–430 mcg/dL (women); >700 suggests adrenal androgen excess

effects_on_skin.electrolyte_balance: Regulates sodium/potassium balance; indirectly affects skin hydration.
effects_on_skin.vascular_tone: Modulates cutaneous blood flow via mineralocorticoid receptors.
effects_on_skin.barrier_function: Emerging evidence for role in skin electrolyte and hydration homeostasis.

testing.serum_aldosterone: Morning sample, after sodium loading or upright posture

effects_on_skin.sebum_production: Prolactin receptors present in sebaceous glands; stimulates sebocyte proliferation and sebum production.
effects_on_skin.adrenal_androgens: Increases adrenal androgen production (DHEA-S) → acne, hirsutism, hair loss.
effects_on_skin.postpartum_changes: Drives melasma persistence, postpartum telogen effluvium, and variable acne.

testing.serum_prolactin: AM, fasting; <25 ng/mL (women); elevated with stress, hypothyroidism, prolactinoma
